req.title: Reset employee password — variant 4
req.statement: An employee shall reset their password via an email link that expires after 30 minutes; expired links must be rejected. Additionally, the operation must be rate-limited per user.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers four orthogonal concerns: (1) successful password reset with a valid token, (2) rejection of expired tokens (410), (3) rejection of policy-violating passwords (422), (4) rate-limiting per user, and (5) session invalidation post-reset. EP partitions tokens into valid/expired/malformed and passwords into policy-compliant/non-compliant. BVA targets the 30-minute expiry boundary. STC covers the state machine: token-issued → token-used (password updated, sessions killed) and token-issued → token-expired → rejected. NEG covers expired token and bad password. DT covers the rate-limit rule combining attempts and time window. The violation fix: test[4] had kind=negative with heuristic=STC; rate-limiting is a state-transition concern (within-limit state → over-limit state) so heuristic must be DT (decision table: attempts × time-window → allow/block), and kind must remain negative since the expected outcome is a rejection (429). All other tests are preserved unchanged.

tests.name: test_reset_password_valid_token_succeeds
preconditions: Employee account exists with known credentials
A fresh (non-expired) password-reset token has been issued for that account
The new password satisfies the password policy
actions: POST /password/reset with valid token and policy-compliant new password
Attempt to authenticate with the old password
Attempt to authenticate with the new password
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE = "http://hr-app"

def test_reset_password_valid_token_succeeds(valid_reset_token, employee_credentials, http_session):
    token = valid_reset_token["token"]
    old_pw = employee_credentials["password"]
    new_pw = "NewP@ssw0rd_Valid1"

    resp = http_session.post(
        f"{BASE}/password/reset",
        json={"token": token, "password": new_pw}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.json().get("updated") is True

    old_auth = http_session.post(
        f"{BASE}/auth/login",
        json={"username": employee_credentials["username"], "password": old_pw}
    )
    assert old_auth.status_code in (401, 403), "Old password must no longer work"

    new_auth = http_session.post(
        f"{BASE}/auth/login",
        json={"username": employee_credentials["username"], "password": new_pw}
    )
    assert new_auth.status_code == 200, "New password must authenticate"

    <teardown_reset_employee_password>(employee_credentials, old_pw)

tests.name: test_reset_password_expired_token_rejected
preconditions: A reset token that has been artificially expired (or naturally expired after 30 min) is available
actions: POST /password/reset with the expired token and a policy-compliant new password
expected: Response body indicates token has expired
trace: R-WEB-105: expired links must be rejected
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE = "http://hr-app"

def test_reset_password_expired_token_rejected(expired_reset_token, employee_credentials, http_session):
    token = expired_reset_token["token"]
    new_pw = "NewP@ssw0rd_Valid1"

    resp = http_session.post(
        f"{BASE}/password/reset",
        json={"token": token, "password": new_pw}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 410, f"Expected 410, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "expired" in body.get("error", "").lower() or "expired" in body.get("message", "").lower()

    auth = http_session.post(
        f"{BASE}/auth/login",
        json={"username": employee_credentials["username"], "password": employee_credentials["password"]}
    )
    assert auth.status_code == 200, "Original password must still work after rejected reset"

tests.name: test_reset_password_at_30min_boundary_expires
preconditions: A reset token whose issued-at timestamp is exactly 30 minutes ago (boundary) is available via test helper
actions: POST /password/reset with a token issued exactly 30 minutes ago and a policy-compliant new password
expected: HTTP 410 Gone returned (token at expiry boundary is treated as expired)
trace: R-WEB-105: email link expires after 30 minutes

tests.name: test_reset_password_policy_violation_rejected
preconditions: A fresh valid reset token has been issued
New password does NOT meet policy (e.g., too short, no special character)
actions: POST /password/reset with valid token and policy-violating new password
expected: Response body lists the specific policy violation(s)
Token is not consumed (can still be used)

tests.name: test_reset_password_rate_limit_enforced
preconditions: Rate-limit threshold N for /password/reset is known (retrieved from config fixture)
N fresh valid tokens are available, or the endpoint accepts repeated attempts with the same token
actions: POST /password/reset N times with valid tokens and policy-compliant passwords within the allowed window
POST /password/reset one additional time (N+1) within the same window
expected: First N requests each return 200 or appropriate per-attempt status
The (N+1)th request returns 429 Too Many Requests
Retry-After header is present in the 429 response
trace: R-WEB-105: operation must be rate-limited per user
